observations.recommendation: (1) We recommended that Management require the GSO, Accounting Office, and other concerned offices to implement the prescribed procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to extinguish or at least reduce by a significant amount the existing huge variance between the physical count and accounting records of PPE.
observations.reason: This matter was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/ activities and limited staff

observations.recommendation: (2) a) Require the concerned officers and employees to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances and strictly adhere to the relative rules and regulations on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances; (3) b) Cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who are still in government service or facilitate effecting the same, until the unliquidated cash advances were settled; (4) c) Observe compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-004, particularly in relation to the accountable officers already separated from government service and whose unliquidated cash advances remained unsettled as of December 31, 2011; (5) d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the previous cash advance of the Disbursing Officer is settled first before a grant of additional cash advance is allowed; and (6) e) Refrain from entering into agreements with accountable officers for salary deductions to settle their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
This cannot be fully implemented given the series of activities in the Municipality with only one Special Disbursing Officer.
Management stopped entering into agreements with accountable officers for salary deductions.
observations.reason: Similar observations were observed during CY 2024 relative to cash advances totaling ₱621,978.78 granted to various officers and employees, and ₱4,113,574.52 granted to the Special Disbursing Officer, which remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2024. As consideration, Management allows settlement through salary deduction, but still grants additional cash advances to accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: (7) a) Direct the personnel concerned to closely monitor the Municipality’s expenditures to avoid cash overdraft; (8) b) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that monies held in trust be used only for the intended purpose; and (9) c) Refrain from disbursing funds for expenditures without the corresponding appropriation therefor; and incurring obligations when no cash is available to cover the same.
observations.reason:
Similar observations and recommendations are reiterated in Part II of this Report.
Not implemented
Not implemented

observations.recommendation: (11) a) Ensure that a Memorandum of Agreement be secured first as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, before undertaking projects involving inter-agency fund transfers; (12) b) Instruct the personnel concerned to conduct periodic analysis, review, and monitoring of all fund transfers to ensure compliance with all requirements stipulated in the MOA as well as in other relevant laws and regulations; (13) c) Require the Municipal Accountant to individually verify and validate the Subsidiary Ledger account balances relating to various fund transfers, especially the prior years’ balances, to establish obligations therefrom and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries, if warranted; and (14) d) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to return any unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned, as stipulated in the MOA.
observations.reason:
Fund transfers totaling ₱512,685.41 remained unliquidated/unreturned/unadjusted as of December 31, 2024, as discussed in Part II of this Reports.
This matter was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/ activities
This matter was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/ activities
Long-outstanding fund balances totaling ₱330,164.95 remained unliquidated/unreturned due to the absence of supporting documents, while funds totaling ₱164,754.18 had not been remitted to the concerned NGAs, as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: (15) a) The Municipal Engineer to ensure that detailed engineering investigations, surveys, and designs, for the project, have been sufficiently carried out and approved; and contract plans and program of works prepared before the preparation of the Annual Investment Program; and (16) b) The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to periodically monitor the implementation of the programs, projects, or activities funded from the 20 percent Local Development Fund.
observations.reason: This matter was not given sufficient time due to limited personnel.

observations.recommendation: (17) We recommended that Management direct the MDRRMO and other concerned personnel to fast-track the implementation of programs, projects, or activities funded under the LDRRMF, particularly those intended for disaster prevention/ mitigation and preparedness, to strengthen the capacities of the Municipality in mitigating the risks and preparing for the impacts of disasters.
observations.reason: Similar observations were noted during CY 2024 such as non-implementation of Dredging/Desilting of Rivers, Creeks and Canals, and Simulation Exercises and Drills on Earthquake, Fire, Flood and Terrorism

observations.recommendation: (21) We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel to submit the lacking documents and ensure that all future disbursements are accompanied by complete documentation before processing the claims therefor, as outlined in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: This recommendation was overlooked due to numerous reports/activities and limited staff.

observations.recommendation: (23) We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to expedite the preparation of the 10-year solid waste management plan pursuant to Section 16 of RA 9003. We further recommended that said plan be properly implemented to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: A 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was prepared and submitted to the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) for review.

observations.recommendation: (25) a) Fully implement all the planned GAD programs, projects, and activities within the target implementation period to ensure utilization of at least five percent of the annual budget to effectively attain the LGU’s GAD objectives; and (26) b) Prepare GAD AR following the procedures indicated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 (as amended). (27) We likewise recommended that Management install a defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on gender mainstreaming.
observations.managementAction: A GAD AR was prepared following the procedures indicated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.
observations.reason:
The GAD PPAs for CY 2024 were likewise not fully implemented, and the budget was underutilized.
No GAD M&E Team has been established.

observations.recommendation: (28) We recommended that Management enjoin the GSO Designate, Municipal Accountant, and other concerned department heads, to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.reason: This recommendation was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/activities and limited staff.

observations.recommendation: (29) a) Accounting Office and the GSO to conduct reconciliation of the amount of the Office Supplies Inventory and ensure that SLCs and SCs are maintained, a physical count of inventories is done semestrally, and the amounts reported in the RPCI tally with the GL account balances; and (30) b) Municipal Accountant to record expenses only when inventories are issued based on the weekly consolidated RIS together with the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) submitted by the GSO, observing proper use of account titles guided by the rules provided in Section 4(s), Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason:
This recommendation was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/activities and limited staff.
This recommendation was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/activities and limited staff.

observations.recommendation: (31) We recommended that Management require the MHO, GSO, and Accounting Office to coordinate with each other to align their inventory control procedures with existing rules and regulations; and facilitate the proper recording of the COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies in the books of accounts as well as in other inventory records.
observations.reason: This recommendation was not given sufficient time due to numerous reports/activities and limited staff.

observations.recommendation: (32) We recommended that Management facilitate the submission of the certifications required under Paragraph B (5) of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 dated December 28, 2021, to support the payment of claims for travel expenses by the JO/COS personnel.
observations.reason: This recommendation was overlooked due to numerous reports/activities and limited staff.

observations.recommendation: (33) a) Install and maintain a system of tax mapping showing graphically all properties subject to assessment in the Municipality, and make frequent physical surveys to check and determine whether all real properties within the locality are properly listed in the assessment rolls; (34) b) Re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities; (35) c) Create new positions and/or provide capable personnel who shall be in charge of the issuance of Notice of Delinquencies (NOD) and the monitoring of the delinquent taxpayers; and
observations.managementAction: Barangay Treasurers were deputized, but for the collection of community tax only.
observations.reason: No relevant position has been created.

observations.recommendation: (37) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in her capacity as the Chairperson of the LDRRMC, instruct the Municipal DRRM Officer and other personnel concerned to conduct in-depth planning of programs, projects, or activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP observing strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 12 of R.A. 10121.
observations.reason: This recommendation has not been implemented as manifested by the non-implementation of some PPAs embodied in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: (38) We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to adopt proper alternative modes of procurement such as the use of Negotiated Procurement- Direct Retail Purchase in the procurement of fuel, oil, and lubricants, as may be warranted by the situation.
observations.reason: The “Negotiated Procurement-Direct Retail Purchase” has not been adopted in the procurement of fuel, oil, and lubricants

observations.recommendation: (39) a) Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the development projects, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; (40) b) Evaluate thoroughly the proposed priority development projects to be funded under the 20% DF as to their readiness for implementation or procurement to avoid delays or non-implementation thereof; and (41) c) Direct the LGU’s Engineer and the Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor periodically the implementation of development projects funded under the 20% DF.
observations.reason: These recommendations were not given sufficient time due to limited personnel in the Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: (42) We recommended that Management direct the officers concerned to adhere with the inventory control process provided under Sections 114 and 121 the MNGAS for LGUs in order to complete the pertinent documents to facilitate the proper handling, timely recording and prompt documentation of inventory costs. (43) We likewise recommended that Management designate a permanent personnel in charge of monitoring the receipt and issuance/ utilization of inventories.
observations.reason: These recommendations were not given sufficient time due to limited personnel.

observations.recommendation: (45) We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the affected accounts to determine the cause of the unreconciled difference amounting to ₱1,137,896.26 and effect the necessary adjustments thereon if warranted.
observations.reason: This recommendation was not given due time.

observations.recommendation: (47) We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspensions/ Disallowances.
observations.reason: The disbursement vouchers were partially submitted.

observations.recommendation: (48) We recommended that Management require the refund of the total amount of ₱1,422,693.78 for the payment of salaries and allowances of the Municipal Administrator which were incurred without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: A total amount of ₱157,678.15 has been collected.

observations.recommendation: (49) a) ensure that payment of additional benefits to its officials and employees is paid in accordance with approved Administrative Order from the Office of the President; and (50) b) require the refund of the additional benefits received by the officials and employees ranging from ₱2,708.33 to 13,000 each or a total amount of ₱1,134,208.33 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: No response/comment was provided by Management on this issue.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) facilitate the immediate implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts, in accordance with the guidelines set forth under COA Circular
No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to enhance the reliability and
accuracy of PPE records;
b) appoint a full-time General Services Officer (GSO) who shall be
responsible for maintaining complete and updated Reports on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Property Cards
(PCs) to support the timely reconciliation of PPE accounts; and
c) establish adequate internal control systems for effective PPE management,
including the conduct of regular physical inventory and reconciliation
procedures, in compliance with the MNGAS for LGUs.

Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

(10) We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines prescribed under Item 6.1 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 particularly on the grant of cash advances to SDO for confidential funds and immediately submit the required documents to support the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were submitted to the Audit Team

observations.recommendation: (18) a) Comply with the required public notices, as well as its specifications and prohibitions, of projects/programs/activities of the LGU, as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 (as amended); (19) b) Inform the OIC-Audit Team Leader within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team; and (20) c) Stop the installation of additional signage without legal basis to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary expenses and redundancy of public notices.

(22) We recommended that Management direct the personnel concerned to ensure that the amount of VAT reflected in the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) for infrastructure contracts conforms with the five percent rate prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 197 s. 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: (24) We recommended that Management ensure that the premium contributions and loan payments withheld from employees’ compensation are duly and timely remitted to the HDMF and PhilHealth within the period prescribed by the pertinent laws, rules, and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management has ensured significant compliance with the timely remittance of contributions to HDMF and PhilHealth, though remittances related to salary adjustments are inevitably delayed.

observations.recommendation: (36) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed analysis on the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and its related Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries pertaining to the unreconciled accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: RPT/SET Receivable accounts and their related Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts are reconciled in the books.

observations.recommendation: (44) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in her capacity as the Co-Chairperson of the Local School Board, facilitate the inclusion of programs, projects or activities related to Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) Programs in the Annual Budget of the Special Education Fund for the ensuing year, to observing strict compliance with the provisions of R.A. 10410 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1, series of 2017.
observations.managementAction: Annual Budget of the Special Education Fund now includes programs, projects or activities related to Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD)

observations.recommendation: (46) We recommended that Management stop the practice of paying supplier/dealer/payee through cash from the concerned officers/personnel which are intended to be reimbursed later. observations.managementAction: Management refrained from reimbursing expenses for purchases subject to procurement

observations.recommendation: Execute all PPAs within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely deliver to the constituents the benefits of the Municipality’s socio- economic and environmental development projects.

observations.recommendation:
Include in the annual LDRRMFIP not only those PPAs chargeable against the current year’s LDRRMF but also disaster-related activities to be funded from previous years’ unexpended balances of the Fund, pursuant to Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 124 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1; and

a) Ensure that the realignment of 20% DF to entirely new PPAs shall effect changes in the Annual Budget through enactment of an ordinance authorizing a supplemental budget in accordance with Section 321 of Local Government Code; and

b) Avoid payments on reimbursement basis, but instead, make use of the alternative modes of procurement prescribed under Sections 48-53 of the IRR of RA 9184.

No stockroom available

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the:

Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Treasurer the updated assessment rolls for the year;  

Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of certified list of taxpayers including the amount due and collectible during the year as a basis in setting up the receivable accounts; and 

Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjusting entries to adjust the balances of RPT/SET Receivables as well as their contra accounts.
observations.reason: Assessment rolls were submitted to Treasurer’s Office last March  29, 2021 only

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Designate one regular personnel each from property and accounting departments who shall reconcile on a full time basis, the submitted Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the accounting records to identify any discrepancies that may require adjustments; and maintain and update the respective property records of all items of PPE; and

Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct the inventory-taking on a full time basis within a specified period to come up with a reconciled, updated and complete RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: Still looking for qualified personnel who can prepare the required PPE records and reports.
observations.reason: There are no qualified personnel who will handle the reconciliation of Accounting and GSO reports on a full time basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the: 

Municipal Accountant to ensure that the JEV recording the expenditures are supported with documents such SSMI or Distribution Lists (duly signed by recipients thereof, date of receipt and quantity indicated); signed attendance/registration sheets, pictures of the activity, and other documents necessary to prove the consumption/usage of those items directly delivered to the requisitioning office;

Coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials and maintain the prescribed records to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies; and

Provision of a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories.
observations.managementAction: For 2021, we will follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of supplies and materials. SSMI or distribution lists will be the basis for the recording of Expenses. We will do it per department, as we do not yet have a stock room.
observations.reason: No stockroom available

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

ensure that payment of additional benefits to its officials and employees is paid in accordance with approved Administrative Order from the Office of the President; and

require the refund of the additional benefits received by the officials and employees ranging from ₱2,708.33 to 13,000 each or a total amount of ₱1,134,208.33 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU employees’ association is in the process of establishing a CNA and /or will comply with the requirements needed to be entitled to Performance Based Bonus.
observations.reason: SB issued a Resolution granting the additional benefits to officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to trace the difference and reconcile the reciprocal accounts, and thereafter effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on its financial operation as of a given period of time.
observations.managementAction: Still looking for additional qualified personnel for Accounting Office.
observations.reason: This was not given due time due to many reports needed by different agencies and due to lack of qualified personnel in the Accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Inventory Committee to include all properties in the RPCPPE and Property/Equipment Cards and ensure that the reports contain adequate details/description, property number, remarks on the condition and whereabouts of PPE and other information necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation of records;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated PPE Ledger Cards in order to completely account for each item composing the PPE, investigate on the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and
Direct both the GSO and Accounting Office to exert all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon may be immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide correct amounts of depreciation and amortization for the identified PPE and intangible assets, respectively and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books. Unrecorded prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity, and the current year’s depreciation should be recorded as expense.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a stock room for proper storage of supplies and materials;
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the semi-annual physical inventory of all supplies and materials inventory of the LGU and prepare the report thereon, copy furnished the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
Facilitate the immediate completion of documents to support the recording of expenses for materials and supplies which has already been distributed and utilized.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Draw the journal voucher to record the valid reconciling items in order to adjust the balance of the cash in bank account after taking into consideration the supporting documents and computations, if any;
Observe timeliness in the preparation and submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements; and
Determine the cause of the difference between the balances of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and the General Ledger amounting to ₱27,232.31 and effect the necessary adjustments thereon.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting journal entries to reclassify the fund transfers from Due to NGAs and other LGUs to their appropriate liability accounts, and observe the proper use of accounts when recording transactions as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that Management exert effort in limiting their PS expenses by identifying and funding only items that are of priority concerns so that adequate funds can be allocated for the implementation of other planned development projects beneficial to the constituents.

We recommended that Management, through their responsible Municipal personnel:
Require every contractor of the infrastructure project to prepare an “As Built” Plan, a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built of the completed project and submit the same within the timeliness of submission;
Prepare and approve the Statement of Work Accomplished based on contractor-prepared As-Built Plan; and
Facilitate the submission of copies of Warranty Securities for the completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management:
Include in the annual LDRRMFIP not only those PPAs chargeable against the current year’s LDRRMF but also disaster-related activities to be funded from previous years’ unexpended balances of the Fund, pursuant to Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 124 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1; and
Prepare the LDRRMFIP observing substantial adherence with the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, thereby making the fund validly available for disbursement.

We recommended that Management ensure that at least 5% of the total annual budget is allocated for programs, projects or activities, embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget and incorporated in the Municipality’s Annual Budget so that it can have sufficient fund to address gender-related issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in her capacity as co-chairperson of the Local School Board ensure the proper planning and utilization of programs/projects/activities charged against the Special Education Fund and refrain from disbursing the fund for PPAs which has no corresponding appropriation therefor.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;

Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, liquidation and utilization of cash advances, if warranted; and

Ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Will prepare a memo stating that no other claims except salaries will be processed unless outstanding cash advance is not yet settled
observations.reason:
We still grant cash advance to Special Disbursing Officer even if she has outstanding cash advance.

We only have one Special Disbursing Officer. There are times when activities/programs of the municipality were done simultaneously.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately return the unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned or request for appropriate authority to use said funds.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspensions/Disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Still trying to retrieve the other documents
observations.reason: Other vouchers and supporting documents cannot be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that GAD activities are implemented in accordance with the approved GAD plan utilizing the funds allotted therefore to address gender issues within the agency as mandated.
observations.reason: Due to pandemic

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and 

Ensure that all EDF projects proposed in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU be fully implemented within the budget year.
observations.managementAction:
Still looking for qualified personnel.
Prepared schedule of the projects to be implemented
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
Lack of qualified personnel in the Engineering office

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;

Create new positions and/or provide capable personnel who shall be in-charge with the issuance of Notice of Delinquencies (NOD) and the monitoring of the delinquent tax payers; and

Intensify the tax campaigns by sending treasury personnel to barangay especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time and maximize collections especially on delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments supported with complete documentation in the respective books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA and other affected accounts.

We recommended that Management require the refund of the total amount of ₱1,422,693.78 for the payment of salaries and allowances of the Municipal Administrator which were incurred without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: Will send demand letter to the concerned person. Balance to be refunded as of December 31, 2020 is P 698,748.23.
observations.reason: The concerned official was separated from service effective July 1, 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit the approved MDC and SB Resolutions for the 20% Development Fund showing the list of projects broken down in details indicating the specific name and location as mandated by Sangguniang Panlalawigan and strictly adhere to various specific laws and circulars mentioned above.

We recommended that Management require that payments for expenses not attributable to current year’s appropriation be stopped and any unpaid disbursements of previous year be reported as part of Accounts Payable.
We recommended that Management eventually switch to sanitary landfill.

We recommended strict compliance to Section 114 of COA Circular No. 2003-002, to enhance the responsibility and accountability of officers and employees tasked to safeguard the assets of the government in general, and supplies and materials inventories in particular.

Dr.: Communications Equipment 
       ₱215,675.00

       ₱215,675.00

To book up the purchase of 12 units cellular phones.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


   a) Stop  the  granting  of  additional  cash  advances  to  officers  and
      employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;


   b) Institute appropriate legal  actions/sanctions  against  those  erring
      accountable  officers/employees  for  continued  non-compliance   with
      existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, liquidation  and
      utilization of cash advances, if warranted; and

   c) Ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
      is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management direct the:


   a. Municipal Assessor to furnish  the  Municipal  Treasurer  the  updated
      assessment rolls for the year;
   b. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the  Municipal  Accountant  a  copy  of
      certified list of taxpayers including the amount due  and  collectible
      during the year as a basis in setting up the receivable accounts; and
   c. Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjusting entries  to  adjust
      the balances of RPT/SET Receivables as well as their contra accounts.

We recommended that Management:


   a. designate one regular personnel  each  from  property  and  accounting
      departments who shall reconcile on a full time  basis,  the  submitted
      Report of Physical Count of Property,  Plant  and  Equipment  (RPCPPE)
      with the accounting records to identify  any  discrepancies  that  may
      require adjustments; and maintain and update  the respective  property
      records of all items of PPE; and


   b. create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct  the  inventory-taking
      on a full time basis within a specified  period  to  come  up  with  a
      reconciled, updated and complete RPCPPE.

We recommended that Management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
immediately return the unutilized funds to the  NGAs  concerned  or  request
for appropriate authority to use said funds.

We recommended that Management require the:


   a) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the JEV recording the expenditures
      are supported with documents such SSMI  or  Distribution  Lists  (duly
      signed by recipients thereof, date of receipt and quantity indicated);
      signed attendance/registration sheets, pictures of the  activity,  and
      other documents necessary to  prove  the  consumption/usage  of  those
      items directly delivered to the requisitioning office;


   b) coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
      Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual  inventory  method
      in recording of supplies and materials  and  maintain  the  prescribed
      records to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in  the
      receipt and issuance of supplies; and


   c) provision of a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories.

We recommended that Management direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
submit immediately the disbursement  vouchers  together  with  its  complete
supporting documents in order that a proper review of  transactions  can  be
achieved to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspensions/Disallowances.

We  recommended  that  Management  ensure  that  GAD  activities   are
implemented in accordance with the approved GAD  plan  utilizing  the  funds
allotted therefore to address gender issues within the agency as mandated.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;

Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance   with   e existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, liquidation and  utilization of cash advances, if warranted; and

Ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No.       97-002.
observations.managementAction: Sent demand letters to accountable officers/employee s.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this report.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to require the completion of the projects as soon as possible and impose appropriate sanctions and penalties, if applicable, as well as intensify the monitoring of project implementation to ensure that projects are completed within the period indicated in the contract.

We recommended that Management:

re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;
create new positions and provide capable personnel that will be in-charge with the issuance of NOD and the monitoring of the delinquent taxpayers; and
intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

strictly follow the policies and guidelines on overtime services and overtime pay; and
require the refund of excess payment totaling ₱80,924.25 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND).

We recommended that Management study the packages offered by mobile networks and evaluate which would provide the best price favorable to the government. We likewise recommend that Management refrain from paying excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it that claims are supported with adequate documentation.
We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the abovementioned regulations and immediately submit the necessary documents to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management directs the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations.

We recommended that Management:

prepare policy guidelines on the availment of the subsidy to Other LGUs; and
require from the grantee barangays the liquidation of the fund transfers/financial assistance.

We recommended that Management require the adequate monitoring of all billing statements and ensure that issued checks be immediately presented for payment to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenses such as penalties and surcharges.
We recommended that Management limit the charged to SEF expenditures to those aligned with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that the objectives of the fund are achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit the approved MDC and SB Resolutions for the 20% Development Fund showing the list of projects broken down in details indicating the specific name and location as mandated by Sangguniang Panlalawigan and strictly adhere to various specific laws and circulars mentioned above.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require that payments for expenses not attributable to current year’s appropriation be stopped and any unpaid disbursements of previous year be reported as part of Accounts Payable.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) prepare policy guidelines on the availment of the subsidy to Other LGUs; and (b) require from the grantee barangays the liquidation of the fund for the transfers/financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of transfers/financial assistance was only implemented for 1 quarter of the year due to monitoring.
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) adhere to the provisions of the abovementioned COA Circular regarding the accounting of LDRRMF; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that full disclosure of the amount and detailed of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to FS of the Municipality at year-end and a Trust Liability-DRRM be established.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented our recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the adequate monitoring of all billing statements and ensure that issued checks be immediately presented for payment to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenses such as penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: Signatories are not always available when releasing checks for utility payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management limit the charged to SEF expenditures to those aligned with the provisions of Section 272 of Republic Act No. 7160, thus, ensure that the objectives of the fund are achieved.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9003; (b) implement the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan they had prepared; (c) put up receptacles that are well categorized and segregated; (d) take appropriate actions that would lead to the closure of the open dump; and (e) eventually switch to sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: No sanitary landfill is available to be used as a dumpsite.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended strict compliance to Section 114 of COA Circular No. 2003-002, to enhance the responsibility and accountability of officers and employees tasked to safeguard the assets of the government in general, and supplies and materials inventories in particular. Likewise, we recommended the following entry to book up the accounts related to the above purchases:
Communications Equipment P215,675.00
Government Equity P215,675.00
To book up the purchase of 12 units cellular phones.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: No justification was given by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management purchase Office Supplies for stock following Section 51 of the New Government Accounting System, Volume I, limiting the supply up to a three-month period, to ensure the availability of Office Supplies and to minimize cost.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented our recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Local officials concerned are advised to adhere to the provisions of Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445. Please be reminded that non-rendition of accounts is an act punishable by law as above stated.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented our recommendations.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management create an internal policy relative to the AICS program so that more and those constituents who are really in need will be benefited by the program.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: Management has yet to create an internal policy on the grant of AICS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Muricipal Accountant to: (a)
facilitate the preparation and/or submission of the monthly bank reconciliation
statements; and (b) prepare the necessary adjustments to record the reconciling
items affecting the aceounts.

We recommended that Management:

(a) stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers and
employees unless the previous ones are fully settied;

(b) institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
accountable officers/cmployees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws,
rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if
warranted; and

(c) ensure that the granting utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in
accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of
granting additional cash advances for Intelligence Funds without the reguired
supporting documents in strict adhercnce to DBM/DILG/GCG/DND Joint Circular
No. 2015-001.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to trace the
difference and reconcile the r2ciprocal accounts, and thereafter effect adjustments
to reflect the correct account balances aud present reliable data on its financial
operation as of a given period of time.

We recommended that Maragement (a) create an Inventory Committee to
facilitate the inventory count of all the LGU’s assets; and (b) reconcile the report
with the accounting records w identify any discrepancies that may require
adjustment.

We recommended and the Management agieed te require the Accountant to
reclassify the costs of completed projects to the appropriate assets account and
derecognize the amount of P16,099,417.16 frem the CIP-TA account.

We recommended that Managemeni:

(a.) strictly foliow the pelicics and guidelines on overtime services and
overtime pay; and

(b.) require the refund of excess payment totaling £80,924.25 to forestall the
issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND).

We recommended that Management study the packages offered by mobile
networks and evaluate which weuld provide the best price favorable to the
government. We likewise recominend that management refrain from paying
excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it
that claims are supported with adequatc documentation.

We recommended and the Management promised to comply with our
recommendations to instruct the Department Heads to:

(a.) stop iminediately the procurement of goods and services om
reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application;

(b.) sec to it that documentary requirements such as Certificates of
Emergency Purchase and completely dated and filled-up Acceptance and Inspection
Report (AIR), Summary .of Expenses and other necessary documents on
procurcment are attached to the Disbursement Youcher (DV), where applicable;
and

(c.) adopt alternative modes of procurement where practicable.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the abovementioned
regulations and immediately submit the necessary decuments to forestall the
issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management dirccts the Bids and Award Committee
(BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the
Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with
existing regulations.

Yo premptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as cmbodied in
the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic
services to the people, we recommended that Management:

(a.) require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the
programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance
with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC Ne. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

(b.) prosecute the projects through straight contract with terms and
conditions indicating definite date of compietion,
